title: Le Monde's point-by-point breakdown of the fragile US-Iran framework agreement
subhead: After more than 100 days of war, the United States and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending their conflict. The document will be followed by negotiations to address issues it does not resolve. Le Monde publishes the full text, along with context and analysis.

title: Russia's shadow looms behind several sabotage and espionage attempts on French soil

Andy Burnham hopes Makerfield by-election win paves his way to Downing Street
subhead: The mayor of Greater Manchester is increasingly emerging as the likely successor to Keir Starmer at the head of Labour – and the next government.

title: After US signals another review of its military presence in Europe, allies grapple with Washington's ambiguities
subhead: On June 18, the US defense secretary announced a 'review' of the distribution of American NATO forces on the continent. The decision has raised concerns, even though it is being presented as a simple 'adjustment,' a senior US defense official told Le Monde.

title: USA down Australia 2-0 to reach World Cup knockout rounds
subhead: The Americans have made a dream start to the tournament with Friday's victory following a 4-1 demolition of Paraguay in their opening game last week. Co-host Mexico has also sealed its place in the knockout rounds after beating South Korea 1-0 on Thursday.

title: 'They call us misfits, degenerates': Free parties fall under French government scrutiny
subhead: Thousands of people gathered on June 6 in the south of France for a rave party, despite the events being banned locally until the end of the year. Participants defend them as among the last spaces of freedom outside of commercial and institutional frameworks.

In Trump's polarized America, empathy faces unanimous opposition
subhead: Once valued in American public life, empathy now faces criticism from the far right, which sees it as a sign of weakness. The left, too, is questioning its relevance: In a world where brutality prevails, can we still afford to show understanding?

title: Trump welcomed at Versailles after G7: 'Macron has turned the château into a diplomatic extension of the Elysée'
subhead: In an interview, historian Fabien Oppermann said that the dinner planned for Wednesday highlights the French president's desire 'to impress his counterpart,' in hopes of preserving the transatlantic relationship at a time when ties with the US are especially strained.

title: French security replaces Palantir with domestic firm ChapsVision, a complicated choice
subhead: Used since the 2015 terrorist attacks, the controversial American company's data analysis software is set to be gradually replaced by that of a French provider. The long-awaited decision presents a significant technological challenge.
